title: Argument to $close can't be an expression
body:
I see in `verilog.y` that `$close` is only allowed to have an identifier as its argument:
```
        |       yD_FCLOSE '(' idClassSel ')'            { $$ = new AstFClose($1, $3); }
```
while other system tasks are allowed to have an expression:
```
        |       yD_FFLUSH '(' expr ')'                  { $$ = new AstFFlush($1, $3); }

        |       yD_FDISPLAY '(' expr ',' exprDispList ')'       { $$ = new AstDisplay($1,AstDisplayType::DT_DISPLAY, $3, $5); }
```
For multi-channel descriptors (MCDs), I would expect to be able to OR them together.  As IEEE 1800-2017 Section 21.3.1 says, "The $fclose system task [...] closes the file(s) specified by the multichannel descriptor mcd."

Is there a reason that the argument to `$fclose` needs to be an identifier?  Or should the parser be updated to allow an expression?

The attached example ([fclose_expr.tar.gz](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/7690347/fclose_expr.tar.gz)) has this code:
```
$fclose({ 1'd0, h1 | h2 });
```
Icarus Verilog compiles and runs it (as can be seen with `make SIM=icarus`), while Verilator (`make`) gives this:
```
%Error: mod.v:21:15: syntax error, unexpected '{'
   21 |       $fclose({ 1'd0, h1 | h2 });
      |               ^
```
The concatenation is a bit contrived; if you want to remove that and just have `$fclose(f1 | f2)`, that might be more realistic, and is also rejected.

I see that `$fflush` and `$fdisplay` originally were only parsed with identifiers as arguments, until commit 281f4e04e in 2015.  I would guess that `$fclose` was just missed?
code_blocks:
|       yD_FFLUSH '(' expr ')'                  { $$ = new AstFFlush($1, $3); }

        |       yD_FDISPLAY '(' expr ',' exprDispList ')'       { $$ = new AstDisplay($1,AstDisplayType::DT_DISPLAY, $3, $5); }
